formula: I wish/If only + Subject + Past Simple (present wish) OR + had + Past Participle (past regret)
formula_visual: I wish/If only + [PAST SIMPLE, for present regret] OR + [had + past participle, for past regret]
when_to_use: Use 'wish'/'if only' to express regret or a strong desire for something to be different. Use Past Simple for regret about the PRESENT situation, and 'had + past participle' for regret about something in the PAST. 'If only' is more emphatic/emotional than 'wish'.
when_to_use_ar: استخدم wish/if only للتعبير عن ندم أو رغبة قوية في اختلاف شيء. استخدم الماضي البسيط للندم على الحاضر، وhad + التصريف الثالث للندم على الماضي. if only أقوى وأكثر عاطفية من wish.
why_arabic_speakers_struggle: This uses the same 'unreal past tense = present meaning' logic as Second Conditional (already tricky), PLUS an additional past-regret layer using Past Perfect — two layers of unusual tense-meaning mismatch stacked together, which takes deliberate practice to internalize rather than translate literally from Arabic's more direct وليت / أتمنى لو constructions.

examples.en: I wish I spoke French. (present regret — I don't speak French now)
examples.ar: أتمنى إني كنت بتكلم فرنساوي (ندم على الحاضر)

examples.en: If only I had more time today. (present regret, emphatic)
I wish I had studied medicine. (past regret — a past decision I regret now)
examples.ar: أتمنى إني كنت درست طب (ندم على قرار ماضٍ)

examples.en: If only she had told me sooner! (past regret, emphatic)
He wishes he hadn't said that. (past regret, negative)

common_errors.explanation: 'wish' about the present needs Past Simple (the 'unreal' marker), not the actual present tense.

common_errors.wrong: I wish I studied medicine. [meant as a past regret]
common_errors.correct: I wish I had studied medicine.
common_errors.explanation: Regret about a PAST decision needs 'had + past participle' (like Third Conditional), not just Past Simple, which would suggest present regret instead.

common_errors.wrong: If only I will have more time.
common_errors.correct: If only I had more time.
common_errors.explanation: 'if only' follows the same unreal-tense rules as 'wish' — never use 'will' directly after it.

practice_fill_blank.sentence: I wish I ___ (have) a bigger house. [present regret]
If only I ___ (know) about this earlier! [past regret]
She wishes she ___ (not/say) that to him. [past regret]
I wish it ___ (not/rain) right now. [present wish]
If only we ___ (leave) earlier yesterday! [past regret]

quick_rule: Wish/if only about NOW = Past Simple. Wish/if only about a PAST decision/event = had + past participle. 'If only' carries more emotional weight than plain 'wish' but follows identical grammar rules.
quick_rule_ar: wish/if only عن الحاضر = ماضٍ بسيط. wish/if only عن قرار/حدث ماضٍ = had + التصريف الثالث.
mnemonic: WISH steps back ONE tense for NOW-regret, and TWO steps back (had + participle) for THEN-regret — the further back in time the regret, the further back the grammar goes.
connect_to_speaking: Practice with your buddy: share one wish about your life right now ('I wish I...') and one regret about a past decision ('I wish I had...' or 'If only I had...') — notice the different tense forms required for each.
